title: Round Modern Dining Table Base
summary: A boxy 2x6 base with rectangular framing and a center cross brace, built to carry a separately-sourced round tabletop — no octagon-to-circle top building here, just the base.
description:
This is a base-only project — build the rectangular 2x6 frame and its center cross brace, then attach a round tabletop you've already sourced or built separately, rather than building an octagon top and cutting it into a circle as this catalog's other round-table project does. Attach the longer 2x6 boards to the legs first to create a simple rectangle, using pocket holes and glue at every joint.

Then attach the shorter pieces to the remaining legs, and **find the true center of the assembled rectangle before attaching the cross brace pieces** — this center point is what the whole base's stability rests on, since the cross brace is what keeps a round top from feeling like it's balanced on a rectangle underneath it. The plan offers two base sizes (48" and 60"), and if you're building the larger 60" version, the source build intentionally added an extra inch to the base dimensions beyond the plan's original numbers specifically to reduce tipping risk under a bigger top — a real consideration worth keeping if you're building at that size.

Once the base is square and the cross brace is centered and attached, flip the tabletop upside down and attach the base to its underside — a genuinely short final step for a base this simple.

materials.name: Screws for attaching tabletop, 2-1/2" (if top is 1-1/2" thick)
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: 1" added beyond the original plan dimension to reduce tipping.
1" added beyond the original plan dimension to reduce tipping.

steps.title: Build the rectangle
steps.body: Attach the longer 2x6 boards to the legs to create a rectangle, using 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the remaining sides and cross brace
steps.body: Attach the shorter remaining pieces to the legs with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws. Find the center of the rectangle and attach the cross brace pieces to it with 2-1/2" pocket holes and glue.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop
steps.body: Flip the round tabletop upside down and attach the assembled base to its underside with screws sized to your tabletop's thickness.
materials: Screws for attaching tabletop, 2-1/2" (if top is 1-1/2" thick)

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Rectangular boxed base for a round dining table top
